channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Quote of the day by Steve Jobs: "What a computer is to me is the most remarkable tool that we have ever come up with. It's the equivalent of a bicycle for our minds" — how technology has augmented us all
item.description: The Apple co-founder was a huge believer that the technology we're building can augment and extend what we're capable of

item.title: ‘Only engineers keen on solving code': Inside the secretive operation ran by US Army to hack into its own systems – and keep the salespersons outside the building
item.description: Operation Jailbreak was a US Army initiative to improve interoperability between weapons, sensors, and command software – immediate results impress.

item.title: Tesla trouve enfin la solution pour corriger le plus gros défaut de ses caméras
item.description: Les caméras aveugles pourraient devenir un lointain souvenir chez Tesla. La marque américaine a en effet breveté un système d’essuie-glaces inédit permettant de conserver la vision quelle que soit la météo.

item.title: Ambrosia Sky's final act lands on August 6
item.description: It'll come as a free update for players who own the game already.

item.title: I asked Siri if it knew what to expect at WWDC 2026 and its answer was so predictably disappointing — I really hope Apple can fix this
item.description: Apple's WWDC 2026 keynote is hours away, but the current Siri doesn't build much confidence that Apple is ready to deliver the AI goods.

item.title: Signet City is a new 'fungalpunk' RPG from the creator of Citizen Sleeper
item.description: Citizen Sleeper creator Gareth Damian Martin has revealed their latest game, Signet City. The new RPG casts players as a fungal parasite in a dying city.

item.title: Is this the dawn of the Tokenpocalypse?
item.description: We're likely to see more price increases as the big AI companies plan to go public.

item.title: Phablets return? Getac's 8-inch Windows 11 rugged tablet looks like a large smartphone, takes SIM cards, and can make VOIP calls
item.description: Getac ZX80W rugged tablets combine Windows 11 ARM performance, AI processing, and industrial durability for demanding field operations across sectors.
